title: viola × sermenika
intro: Viola × sermenika is a hybrid plant that combines the characteristics of viola and sermenica species, and is commonly known as the 'Tibetan Pansy' or 'Serbian Viola'. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Viola × sermenika are believed to provide relief from various health issues, including fever, coughs, and digestive problems, and it is also used to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Viola × sermenika include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to the affected areas.
